role_in_workflow: Convert biological hypotheses into testable genetic questions
public_description: Defines the candidate exposure, biological axis, measurable molecular layer, and aging-related outcome classes before statistical testing.
creld2_application: Reframed the adipose-liver secretory stress hypothesis as a test of whether genetic regulation of circulating CRELD2 relates to aging-related traits.

role_in_workflow: Determine whether a candidate has sufficient public data for analysis
public_description: Checks the availability and compatibility of eQTL, pQTL, GWAS, allele frequency, sample size, and regional summary statistics.
creld2_application: Selected GTEx v8 tissue eQTLs, UKB-PPP pQTLs, and GWAS outcomes with available summary statistics for frailty, leukocyte telomere length, and parental longevity.

role_in_workflow: Map feasible evidence layers to reproducible scripts
public_description: Specifies deterministic analysis modules for pQTL extraction, allele harmonization, lead-variant Wald ratio MR, and Bayesian colocalization.
creld2_application: Generated the CRELD2 pQTL locus manifest, lead pQTL MR estimates, and coloc.abf() regional colocalization summaries.

role_in_workflow: Identify limitations and run targeted checks when feasible
public_description: Records unresolved design issues, allele harmonization risks, sample overlap concerns, and sensitivity analyses.
creld2_application: Evaluated the palindromic C/G lead variant rs74510325 using pQTL, GWAS, dbSNP/PAGE, and gnomAD allele frequencies, then repeated colocalization excluding palindromic variants.

role_in_workflow: Separate exploratory support from confirmatory causal claims
public_description: Interprets the combined evidence using predefined thresholds and design limitations, including single-instrument MR and potential cohort overlap.
creld2_application: Reported CRELD2 as exploratory genetic support for frailty-related aging biology, not as definitive causal evidence.
